# tokens: # Resource — built-in object type for external data sources
# `Resource` and `Entity` are AQL's built-in ObjectTypes for naming
# external data sources. They are not registered as words; instead
# `installResourceTypes` (called from `engine.Register`) pushes them
# onto the type stack at startup. So they're available out of the
# box wherever the production registry is loaded.
# Hierarchy:
#   Object/Resource           {kind:String}
#       └── Object/Resource/Entity   {spec:String, entity:String}
#                                    (inherits kind from Resource)
# Resource is the abstract carrier; `Entity` adds the (`spec`,
# `entity`) coordinates that pin one specific entity within a
# specifier. Instances are produced via `make Entity {…}` (or via
# user-defined types that extend Resource / Entity).
# This file covers:
#   1. The bare type literals — Resource, Entity.
#   2. Inheritance check — Entity is Resource.
#   3. Instantiation via make.
#   4. Field access on instances.
#   5. is checks for parent / sibling types.
#   6. Field validation at make-time.
# Runs against the production aql registry.
# 1. Bare type literals
Resource
note: the bare type literal renders as its name

# tokens: Entity
typeof Resource
typeof Entity
# 2. Inheritance — Entity is Resource
# `is` between type literals reports whether the LHS type is a
# subtype of the RHS type. Entity declares Resource as parent in
# installResourceTypes, so the check holds.
Entity is Resource
Resource is Resource
# Sibling types do not satisfy each other.
Entity is Map
Entity is List
# 3. Instantiation via make
# `make Entity {…}` validates the data against Entity's fields
# (kind:String + spec:String + entity:String, with kind inherited
# from Resource). Returns a typed object value rendered as
# `Object/Resource/Entity{…}`.
make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'voxgig-solardemo' entity:'planet' } typeof
note: the made value's typeof is Entity

# tokens: make Entity { kind:'db' spec:'users' entity:'user' } typeof
# 4. is checks for the made instance
# An Entity instance satisfies Entity AND its parent Resource —
# inheritance flows through `is` for Type values produced by
# `make`.
make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'x' entity:'y' } is Entity
make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'x' entity:'y' } is Resource
# Cross-family checks fail.
make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'x' entity:'y' } is List
make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'x' entity:'y' } is Integer
# 5. Field access via dotted accessor
# Entity instances expose `kind`, `spec`, and `entity` as fields
# accessible through the standard `.field` syntax.
( make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'voxgig-solardemo' entity:'planet' } ) .kind
( make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'voxgig-solardemo' entity:'planet' } ) .spec
( make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'voxgig-solardemo' entity:'planet' } ) .entity
# 6. Field validation at make-time
# `make Entity {…}` requires every declared field. Missing fields
# raise an error rather than installing a partially-typed value.
make Entity { }
make Entity { kind:'api' }
make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'x' }
# 7. Composition with def
# `def name make Entity {…}` captures an Entity instance for reuse.
# All field accesses and is-checks survive the round-trip through
# the def stack.
def planet ( make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'solardemo' entity:'planet' } ) planet .kind
def planet ( make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'solardemo' entity:'planet' } ) planet .entity
def planet make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'solardemo' entity:'planet' } planet is Entity
def planet make Entity { kind:'api' spec:'solardemo' entity:'planet' } planet is Resource
# 8. Resource and Entity at the type-stack level
# `Resource` and `Entity` are pushed onto the type stack at startup
# (installResourceTypes), so they're always reachable as type
# literals. Subsequent `type NAME …` declarations don't shadow them.
size [ Resource ]
note: the type literal can be wrapped in a list

# tokens: size [ Entity ]
size [ Resource Entity ]
# A user-defined type that USES Resource or Entity as a parent must
# go through a user-named indirection — `type X object {…} Resource`
# does NOT install Resource as the parent because the bare Resource
# type literal does not satisfy `object`'s second-arg slot at parse
# time. Inheriting requires an intermediate user type.
